goal: Celtic full board: collapse the 78-card grid into 4 compact overlapping rows + fix the slow deal
root_cause: The full board used a 4-column grid → 78 cards rendered ~20 rows tall (endless scroll). And animateDealSlots staggers each card by DEAL_STAGGER=160ms, so a 78-card shuffle deal took ~12s (350+78*160+120ms).
fix: full.css §5/§6: switched .card-board--full from a 4-col grid to flex-wrap with cards 34px wide + margin-right:-17px (half-overlap, ~17px step) and max-width 358px → ~20 cards per row → 4 rows (20/20/20/18), board height ~288px. Added z-index on :hover/:focus-visible (4) and .is-selected (6) so an interacted card lifts above its right-neighbour (later siblings paint on top when overlapping); selected lift bumped to -7px. main.js animateDealSlots: for boards >24 slots, shrink the per-card stagger to floor(700/n) (~9ms for 78) so the deal lands in ~0.8s; daily/question (12 cards) keep the original 160ms ceremony.
verified: chrome MCP localhost @430px: measured rowCounts [20,20,20,18], height 288px; selection works (3 picks → badges 1/2/3, counter 3/10, selected z-index 6 lifted above neighbours, non-selected dimmed). node --check passes; md5 parity canonical=www=ios.
notes: Per-card tap target is a ~17px sliver (the trade-off for 20/row as requested); the lift on hover/select makes the active card fully visible. Daily/question boards untouched (different column count + animation profile).

goal: Tap the deck name on the selection board → 'Your deck' picker → choose a deck → board card-backs + label swap to it
fix: main.js: renderDeckName now calls wireDeckSwitcher() — the .daily-topbar__deck / .full-topbar__deck label becomes a role=button with a ▾ caret that opens showDeckPicker(). The picker is a bottom-sheet overlay listing all decks as a 3-col grid (00-back-200 thumbs); the active deck is gold-bordered, locked decks are greyscale + disabled (canUnlockDeck gate). Choosing an owned deck → setActiveDeck(id) → repaintCardBacks() (re-applies applyCardBackBackground to every rendered .card-back — the repaint requirement flagged in CLAUDE.md backlog) → renderDeckName() → close. Styles injected once via ensureDeckPickerStyles(). common.js: new i18n keys deckPickerTitle/deckSwitchAria/deckPickerHint (EN: 'Your deck'/'Change deck'/'Tap a deck to use it on the board'; TH: 'สำรับของคุณ'/'เปลี่ยนสำรับ'/'เลือกสำรับ').
verified: chrome MCP localhost @430px full.html: deck label clickable + caret, picker opens with 11 cells (10 locked logged-out, Moonmallow active); ?deck=sushicat proves the board backs + label honour the active deck (so repaint visually swaps for owned decks). Owned-deck end-to-end swap needs a multi-deck logged-in account to see live. node --check passes; md5 parity canonical=www=ios.
notes: setActiveDeck added to main.js imports. Per-deck ownership still gated by canUnlockDeck, so locked decks can't be activated from the picker.

goal: Remove the confusing 'Deactivate account' (local-reset) button that duplicated the real Delete account
root_cause: renderOther rendered a 'Deactivate account' button that only called resetLocalProgress() (a device-local streak/progress wipe — nothing server-side), and it rendered ungated, so a signed-in user saw it alongside the real 'Delete account' (deleteAccount → delete-account edge fn, Guideline 5.1.1(v)) — two buttons that read as duplicate/conflicting account-removal options.
fix: renderOther now just clears + hides #profile-other (host.hidden=true); removed the deactivate trigger/confirm UI and the now-unused resetLocalProgress import. The logged-in 'Delete account' is the single account-removal path.
verified: chrome MCP profile.html: #profile-other hidden (offsetHeight 0), 0 .profile-deactivate-trigger, body no longer mentions Deactivate/ปิดการใช้งานบัญชี. node --check passes; md5 parity canonical=www.
notes: resetLocalProgress still exported by progress.js (unused now); profileDeactivate* i18n keys left in common.js (harmless, no longer referenced).
